title: Major Account Plan: IBM (excluding Global Services) — Aberdeen Internal Q3 2001
author: Peter S. Kastner, Cindy Murray, Ed Black (Aberdeen Group)
abstract: Aberdeen internal IBM major-account plan covering Q3 2001 status, 18-month goals, and recommended approach. Documents $31K YTD revenue vs $491K prior year; IBM locked-down PO status for two quarters; sales exec Cindy Murray returning from maternity leave. Targets include eLiza, pervasive computing, WebSphere, and leveraging relationship with IBM senior VP Linda Sanford. Flag: Aberdeen-internal confidentiality.
importance_rationale: Unusual primary-source glimpse of an analyst-firm's internal account-management mechanics, revenue targets, and named executive leverage strategy — rarely preserved.
relevance_rationale: Template for account planning, revenue-mix targeting (ETI/retainers/consulting), and senior-sponsor leverage strategy still directly applicable to analyst firms, consultancies, and B2B sales orgs.
prescience_rationale: Prediction of IBM as 'industry survivor' was correct; specific 18-month revenue targets ($750K Q4-2002 run rate) unverifiable from this archive.
